chapter_context.summary: The Sabbath Year and the Year of Jubilee, emphasizing rest and restoration.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Sabbath Year (25:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Leviticus.
This section focuses on The Year of Jubilee (25:8-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Leviticus.
This section focuses on Redemption of Land (25:23-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Leviticus.
This section focuses on Lending to the Poor (25:35-43), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Leviticus.

key_themes.title: Holiness
key_themes.description: The central call to be set apart as God is holy.

key_themes.title: Atonement
key_themes.description: The necessity of sacrifice to reconcile with God.

devotional.question: How does the detailed nature of these laws reflect God's care for every part of our lives?
In what ways do these ancient sacrifices point us toward the ultimate sacrifice of Christ?

cultural_historical.title: The Sacrificial System
cultural_historical.text: Ancient Near Eastern cultures used sacrifices, but the Levitical system was unique in its focus on moral holiness and direct covenant with one God.
